what_it_does: A deterministic Python CLI with two additive modes: a default mockup-scan that reads design tokens, addresses, the feature register, and HTML mockups to report hardcoded literals that should be tokens, candidate new colour tokens, feature-coverage gaps, address orphans, and hygiene misses; and an optional design-lint mode that scans app .tsx/.css source for hardcoded off-token colour (hex/rgba) and optional px literals, plus bespoke elements, and can exit non-zero to gate a build.
observations: Default mode is unchanged; --target triggers the additive design-lint mode.
Mockup scan writes check-report.json; design-lint writes a separate report only if --report is given.
find_literals uses regex for hex (#fff/#ffffff) and px sizes, but not rgba.
candidate_tokens aggregates literal counts across mockups and maps recurring literals to existing token values.
_resolved_token_values resolves token refs against primitives to produce final values.
mockup_scan reports should_use_var, candidate_new_colour_tokens, coverage_gaps, address_orphans, and hygiene (missing design-system.css link or data-ui-ref).
design_lint flags a single occurrence of a hardcoded literal, unlike the mockup scan's recurrence-based finder.
_find_bespoke_elements is a documented stub returning [] until F4/F1 reconciles component inventory.
run_design_lint fails loudly if the target contains no .tsx/.css files or a file cannot be read.
References to 'rule 4' and 'rule 9' appear in error messages without defining them in this file.
standouts: Dual-mode CLI that preserves legacy behaviour while adding a build-gating design-lint.
Literal-to-token candidate finder that both suggests var(--x) replacements and proposes new tokens.
Single-occurrence off-token gating in design-lint versus recurrence-based tokenisation in mockup-scan.
Bespoke-element detection is intentionally stubbed and wired for future activation.
conventions: Uses collections.Counter and defaultdict for aggregation.
Reports are written as indented JSON.
Uses os.path.relpath when printing file paths.
concerns: _find_bespoke_elements is a stub that always returns [], so --fail-on cannot actually catch bespoke elements today.
find_literals in mockup-scan does not detect rgba, while design_lint does, creating an asymmetry.
File reads use open(...) without with-statements.
_resolved_token_values assumes every 'ref' exists in primitives with no fallback.
mockup_scan assumes tokens.json, addresses.json, register.json, and a mockups directory exist without existence checks.
Coverage-gap logic only checks feature ids against view represents lists; it does not validate that represented ids exist as features.
notes: A deterministic, model-free design gate that checks mockup coherence and can optionally lint live app source for off-token literals.
questions: What is the exact schema and content of parse.build_map?
What do tokens.json, addresses.json, and register.json look like beyond the fields used?
What is the intended format and semantics of data-ui-ref attributes?
How are token values normalised so that #fff and #ffffff are compared correctly?
Why does mockup-scan ignore rgba while design-lint flags it?
What are 'rule 4' and 'rule 9' in the project's AGENTS.md?
When and how will the bespoke-element stub be graduated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation closely; the only mismatch is that bespoke-element detection is documented as a stub and not yet functional.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a cheap, exact Layer-0 checker that bridges design tokens, mockups, and live implementation, surfacing tokenisation opportunities and enforcing compliance before any semantic or model-based review.
novelty: Combines a recurrence-based mockup token-candidate miner with a single-occurrence off-token build gate for app source, while explicitly stubbing future bespoke-element detection so it does not introduce false positives.
contribution: Provides a deterministic structural-coherence checker that identifies literal-to-token mappings, proposes new tokens, reports coverage and address orphans, checks mockup hygiene, and offers an additive source-level design-lint gate with loud failure semantics.
summary_for_embedding: A Python CLI that performs deterministic structural coherence checks on design mockups and optionally design-lints live app source files for hardcoded off-token literals.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as a free, exact Layer-0 design gate that catches tokenisation gaps, coverage gaps, address orphans, hygiene omissions, and off-token literals before model-based review.

symbols.does: Finds hardcoded hex colour and px size literals in a text blob using regex.
symbols.description: Returns a list of all matches for #rgb/#rrggbb hex codes and \d+px size literals found in the supplied text. It is used by the mockup-scan candidate-token aggregator and does not detect rgba values.

symbols.does: Aggregates hardcoded literals across mockup screens and maps them to existing token values.
symbols.description: Counts every literal found by find_literals across the mockup corpus, records which screens contain each literal, and checks whether the literal equals an existing token value. Returns sorted records that can drive 'use var(--x)' advice or proposals for new tokens.

symbols.does: Resolves token references to primitive values from a tokens.json structure.
symbols.description: Walks the groups/tokens section of a tokens file, replacing any 'ref' key with the corresponding value from the primitives map, and returns a flat dictionary of token-name to final value.

symbols.does: Yields .tsx and .css files under a directory, glob, or single-file target.
symbols.description: Normalises the design-lint target into an iterable of source file paths, supporting directory traversal, glob patterns, and a single file path. Non-matching targets result in an empty iterator, which callers treat as a failure condition.

symbols.does: Placeholder that currently returns no bespoke-element findings.
symbols.description: A documented stub for C0 that is wired into the design-lint failure count but always returns an empty list. It is intended to become meaningful once data-ui-ref and a component inventory are reconciled in later features.

symbols.does: Scans app source files for hardcoded off-token literals and bespoke elements.
symbols.description: Reads each .tsx/.css file under the target and records every hex, rgba, and optional px literal by file and line, plus any bespoke elements found. It flags single occurrences, distinguishing it from the recurrence-based mockup scan, and returns a structured report.

symbols.does: Executes design-lint, prints a summary, optionally writes a JSON report, and returns an exit code.
symbols.description: Orchestrates the design-lint pass, refuses to silently pass when no source files are found, prints findings, writes a report if requested, and returns a non-zero exit code when --fail-on is set and off-token or bespoke findings exist.

symbols.does: Runs the default mockup structural-coherence scan and writes check-report.json.
symbols.description: Loads tokens, addresses, the feature register, and HTML mockups, then computes literal-to-token candidates, coverage gaps, address orphans, and hygiene issues, prints a summary, and persists the full report to check-report.json.

symbols.does: Dispatches between the default mockup-scan and the optional design-lint CLI modes.
symbols.description: Parses command-line arguments for --target, --fail-on, --include-px, and --report, runs the mockup scan when no target is given, or runs the design-lint gate when a target is provided.
